content.full_text:
State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance • • Administration • • Executive/Legal • • Enforcement • • Statements of Economic Interest
(916) 322-5662 322-5660 322-5901 322-6441 322-6444

November 10, 1981

Al Flor
Assistant County Clerk
San Joaquin County
P.O. Box 810
Stockton, CA 95201

Dear Mr. Flor:

Thank you for your letter dated October 19, 1981, regarding the reporting
of contributions received through the purchase of tickets to fundraising
events.

The purchase of tickets to fundraising events constitutes a contribution.
In our telephone conversation of October 26, 1981, you indicated that the
reference in your letter to "services rendered" was to meals or
refreshments provided at the fundraising event. In determining the amount
of the contribution, the full amount paid for the ticket is used. The
cost of the meal or refreshments provided at the event is not deducted
from the cost of the ticket.

All contributions received by a candidate or committee must be disclosed
on the candidate's or committee's campaign statement. Contributors of a
cumulative amount of $100 or more must be identified on the campaign
statement. The record keeping guidelines (2 Cal. Admin. Code
Section 18401; FPPC Record Keeping Manual) established by the Fair Politi-
cal Practices Commission require that all contributions of $25 or more
be recorded and cumulated with all other contributions received from a single
source for the purpose of determining whether $100 has been received from
that source. All contributions received of less that $100 during the pe-
riod covered by the campaign statement are reported in a lump sum amount.

I hope this adequately answers the questions posed in your letter and the
letter you enclosed from Nancy Buscaglia. If you have any questions
please call me at the above telephone number for the Technical Assistance
and Analysis Division.

Sincerely,

Jeanne Pritchard
Jeanne Pritchard
Political Reform Consultant

JP:st

Enclosure: 2 Cal. Admin. Code Section 18401
Record Keeping Manual

Nancy Buscaglia

[address partially visible] • Stockton, California 95202 • Telephone (209) 931-3890

October 10, 1991

Elections/Registration
County Clerk
119 East Weber Street
Stockton, CA 95202

Dear Mr. Flor:

I am requesting written interpretive rulings from your office on
the reporting requirements of campaign related ticket sales.

I have received three different interpretations from your office.
First, in March from July [illegible] who said "ticket sales do not
need to be reported and receipts of individual ticket sales were
no longer needed." I requested written verification.

In the absence of written ruling from your office, I called George
Barber's campaign treasurer and was advised per Page 14 of the 1991 FPPC Information Manual "ticket sales that
ticket sales should be reported as a contribution.

The June report by George [illegible] committee was rejected by your
office. Your office used the example $1.00 contributors be
omitted because they had purchased two dinner tickets for the
contribution. Mr. Barber's treasurer resubmitted the report per
your instructions.

I am now in the process of auditing reporting guidelines for
Dick Eichenberyer's committee. In a recent telephone
conversation with you I was told "no ticket sales need to be
reported if a service was rendered." Requesting further clarification,
I was referred to you. It is my understanding of our conversation
that if the ticket price exceeds $25 it should be recorded with the
reasonable cost of the service deducted from the ticket
price. The remainder is calculated as the contribution. When the
cumulative is $100 or more per individual or couple, it should be
reported. I requested written confirmation. As yet it has not been
forthcoming.

Until I receive the confirmation, I am advising Richard Willey,
treasurer for the Committee to Elect Dick Eichenberger, to follow
the guidelines set forth in the 1991 manual with the hope that your
office will accept the report.

I appreciate the difficulty of your job in keeping up with the ever
changing regulations, but I must request and understand my need for
definitive written guidelines.

Sincerely,

Nancy Buscaglia

cc: Richard Willey, Committee to Elect Dick Eichenberger
    Kathy Bemme, Committee to Elect [illegible] Mayor

SAN JOAQUIN COUNTY

OFFICE OF THE COUNTY CLERK

RALPH W. EPPERSON
COUNTY CLERK

222 EAST WEBER AVENUE ROOM 202
MAILING ADDRESS P.O. BOX 810
STOCKTON, CALIFORNIA 95201
TELEPHONE (209) 944-3201

October 19, 1981

Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804

Gentlemen:

On two occasions our office has requested guidelines relating to
reporting contributions through the purchase of tickets by one
individual which exceed $100.

Attached is a letter to our office from Nancy Buscaglia who is
treasurer for two campaign committees. It is my opinion and
understanding after my conversation with your office on October
10th that if the ticket price exceeds $25, it should be recorded
with the reasonable cost of the service rendered subtracted from
the ticket price. The remainder is accumulated as the contribution. When the contribution exceeds $100 per individual or
couple, it should be reported.

Inasmuch as the Fair Political Practices Commission is the agency
who finally rules on these matters, would you please make a
written response to the undersigned relating to this question?

As a matter of information, I have attached a copy of the letter
relating to this question.

Very truly yours,

RALPH W. EPPERSON
County Clerk

AL FLOR
Assistant County Clerk

RWE:AF:am

Enclosure

cc: Nancy Buscaglia
